questions.question: A researcher finds that students who exercise more frequently tend to have higher GPAs. Based on this finding alone, we can conclude that:

options.text: Higher GPAs motivate students to exercise more
There is a relationship between exercise frequency and GPA, but we cannot determine causation
A third variable has been ruled out as an explanation

questions.explanation: This is a correlational finding. While it identifies a relationship between exercise and GPA, correlation does not equal causation. A third variable (such as self-discipline or socioeconomic status) could explain both behaviors. Only an experiment with random assignment can establish causation.

questions.question: In an experiment testing whether background music affects reading comprehension, the independent variable is:

options.text: The presence or absence of background music
The participants' age and education level
The difficulty level of the reading passages

questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor that the researcher deliberately manipulates -- in this case, whether background music is present or absent. The dependent variable (reading comprehension scores) is what is measured to see if the manipulation had an effect.
